```{r}
## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Tilburg <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_topology_Tilburg),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Tilburg$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Tilburg$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Tilburg),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_covering", adonis_covering_Tilburg),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_typestreet", adonis_typestraat_Tilburg),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_streetname", streetname_pairwise_Tilburg$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value", streetname_pairwise_Tilburg$F.value),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Tilburg, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Tilburg.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Tilburg"))
```

## ITS PCoA per topology

### Treepit

```{r}
phy_Treepit <- subset_samples(phy_rare, topology=="Treepit")
meta_Treepit <- subset(enriched_meta, topology == "Treepit")

#PCOA Jaccard Treepit-------------
ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit <- ordinate(phy_Treepit, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")


df_jaccard_Treepit <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit$vectors[,1:2], meta_Treepit, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_jaccard_Treepit) <- df_jaccard_Treepit$Row.names
df_jaccard_Treepit <- df_jaccard_Treepit[, -1] 


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all_Treepit <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Treepit, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=city, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Treepit") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Treepit
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Treepit.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary_Treepit <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Treepit, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary_Treepit <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Treepit, FUN=dstats)
df_jaccard_Treepit_mean <- cbind(X_summary_Treepit,Y_summary_Treepit[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_Treepit_mean <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Treepit_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=city, shape = city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Treepit$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Treepit") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Treepit_mean
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Treepit_error_bars.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

## And statistics
# Calculate jaccard-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist_Treepit <- phyloseq::distance(phy_Treepit, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city_Treepit <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Treepit ~ city, data= meta_Treepit)
print(adonis_city_Treepit)
adonis_streetname_Treepit <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Treepit ~ streetname, data= meta_Treepit)
print(adonis_streetname_Treepit)


###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist_Treepit, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

PairPermITS_Treepit <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist_Treepit, meta_Treepit$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS_Treepit
PairPermITS_Treepit$F.value


## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Treepit <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_city_Treepit),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Treepit$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Treepit$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Treepit),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Treepit, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Treepit.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Treepit"))
```

### sidewalk with trees

```{r}
phy_Sidewalk_with_trees <- subset_samples(phy_rare, topology=="Sidewalk_with_trees")
meta_Sidewalk_with_trees <- subset(enriched_meta, topology == "Sidewalk_with_trees")

#PCOA Jaccard Sidewalk_with_trees-------------
ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees <- ordinate(phy_Sidewalk_with_trees, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")


df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees$vectors[,1:2], meta_Sidewalk_with_trees, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees) <- df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees$Row.names
df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees <- df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees[, -1] 


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_with_trees <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=city, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Sidewalk_with_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_with_trees
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Sidewalk_with_trees.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary_Sidewalk_with_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary_Sidewalk_with_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees, FUN=dstats)
df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees_mean <- cbind(X_summary_Sidewalk_with_trees,Y_summary_Sidewalk_with_trees[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_with_trees_mean <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_with_trees_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=city, shape = city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Sidewalk_with_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_with_trees_mean
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Sidewalk_with_trees_error_bars.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

## And statistics
# Calculate jaccard-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_with_trees <- phyloseq::distance(phy_Sidewalk_with_trees, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city_Sidewalk_with_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_with_trees ~ city, data= meta_Sidewalk_with_trees)
print(adonis_city_Sidewalk_with_trees)
adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_with_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_with_trees ~ streetname, data= meta_Sidewalk_with_trees)
print(adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_with_trees)


###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_with_trees, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

PairPermITS_Sidewalk_with_trees <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_with_trees, meta_Sidewalk_with_trees$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS_Sidewalk_with_trees
PairPermITS_Sidewalk_with_trees$F.value


## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Sidewalk_with_trees <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_city_Sidewalk_with_trees),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Sidewalk_with_trees$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Sidewalk_with_trees$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_with_trees),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Sidewalk_with_trees, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Sidewalk_with_trees.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Sidewalk_with_trees"))

```

### Street_with_trees

```{r}
phy_Street_with_trees <- subset_samples(phy_rare, topology=="Street_with_trees")
meta_Street_with_trees <- subset(enriched_meta, topology == "Street_with_trees")

#PCOA Jaccard Street_with_trees-------------
ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees <- ordinate(phy_Street_with_trees, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")


df_jaccard_Street_with_trees <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees$vectors[,1:2], meta_Street_with_trees, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_jaccard_Street_with_trees) <- df_jaccard_Street_with_trees$Row.names
df_jaccard_Street_with_trees <- df_jaccard_Street_with_trees[, -1] 


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all_Street_with_trees <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Street_with_trees, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=city, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Street_with_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Street_with_trees
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Street_with_trees.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary_Street_with_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Street_with_trees, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary_Street_with_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Street_with_trees, FUN=dstats)
df_jaccard_Street_with_trees_mean <- cbind(X_summary_Street_with_trees,Y_summary_Street_with_trees[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_Street_with_trees_mean <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Street_with_trees_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=city, shape = city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_with_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Street_with_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Street_with_trees_mean
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Street_with_trees_error_bars.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

## And statistics
# Calculate jaccard-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist_Street_with_trees <- phyloseq::distance(phy_Street_with_trees, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city_Street_with_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Street_with_trees ~ city, data= meta_Street_with_trees)
print(adonis_city_Street_with_trees)
adonis_streetname_Street_with_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Street_with_trees ~ streetname, data= meta_Street_with_trees)
print(adonis_streetname_Street_with_trees)


###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist_Street_with_trees, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

PairPermITS_Street_with_trees <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist_Street_with_trees, meta_Street_with_trees$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS_Street_with_trees
PairPermITS_Street_with_trees$F.value


## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Street_with_trees <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_city_Street_with_trees),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Street_with_trees$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Street_with_trees$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Street_with_trees),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Street_with_trees, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Street_with_trees.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Street_with_trees"))

```

### Street without trees

```{r}
phy_Street_without_trees <- subset_samples(phy_rare, topology=="Street_without_trees")
meta_Street_without_trees <- subset(enriched_meta, topology == "Street_without_trees")

#PCOA Jaccard Street_without_trees-------------
ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees <- ordinate(phy_Street_without_trees, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")


df_jaccard_Street_without_trees <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees$vectors[,1:2], meta_Street_without_trees, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_jaccard_Street_without_trees) <- df_jaccard_Street_without_trees$Row.names
df_jaccard_Street_without_trees <- df_jaccard_Street_without_trees[, -1] 


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all_Street_without_trees <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Street_without_trees, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=city, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Street_without_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Street_without_trees
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Street_without_trees.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary_Street_without_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Street_without_trees, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary_Street_without_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Street_without_trees, FUN=dstats)
df_jaccard_Street_without_trees_mean <- cbind(X_summary_Street_without_trees,Y_summary_Street_without_trees[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_Street_without_trees_mean <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Street_without_trees_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=city, shape = city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Street_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Street_without_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Street_without_trees_mean
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Street_without_trees_error_bars.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

## And statistics
# Calculate jaccard-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist_Street_without_trees <- phyloseq::distance(phy_Street_without_trees, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city_Street_without_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Street_without_trees ~ city, data= meta_Street_without_trees)
print(adonis_city_Street_without_trees)
adonis_streetname_Street_without_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Street_without_trees ~ streetname, data= meta_Street_without_trees)
print(adonis_streetname_Street_without_trees)


###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist_Street_without_trees, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

PairPermITS_Street_without_trees <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist_Street_without_trees, meta_Street_without_trees$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS_Street_without_trees
PairPermITS_Street_without_trees$F.value


## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Street_without_trees <- bind_rows(
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_city_Street_without_trees),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Street_without_trees$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Street_without_trees$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Street_without_trees),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Street_without_trees, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Street_without_trees.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Street_without_trees"))

```

### Sidewalk without trees

```{r}
phy_Sidewalk_without_trees <- subset_samples(phy_rare, topology=="Sidewalk_without_trees")
meta_Sidewalk_without_trees <- subset(enriched_meta, topology == "Sidewalk_without_trees")

#PCOA Jaccard Sidewalk_without_trees-------------
ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees <- ordinate(phy_Sidewalk_without_trees, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")


df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees <- merge(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees$vectors[,1:2], meta_Sidewalk_without_trees, by = "row.names")
row.names(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees) <- df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees$Row.names
df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees <- df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees[, -1] 


mytheme <- theme_bw()+
  theme(text = element_text(size=15),
        strip.background = element_blank(),
        strip.placement = "outside",
        legend.box.background = element_rect(),
        legend.box.margin = margin(1, 1, 1, 1),
        legend.title = element_text(face = "bold"),
        panel.grid.major = element_blank(), 
        panel.grid.minor = element_blank()) 

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_without_trees <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees, aes(Axis.1,Axis.2, fill=city, shape=city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Sidewalk_without_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_without_trees
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Sidewalk_without_trees.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

#Now I want to add error-bars
dstats <- function(x)(c(n=length(x), mean=mean(x), sd=sd(x), se=sd(x)/sqrt(length(x))))
X_summary_Sidewalk_without_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.1~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees, FUN=dstats)
Y_summary_Sidewalk_without_trees <- summaryBy(Axis.2~city+streetname, data=df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees, FUN=dstats)
df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees_mean <- cbind(X_summary_Sidewalk_without_trees,Y_summary_Sidewalk_without_trees[,3:6])

## And one with Error bars
pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_without_trees_mean <- ggplot(df_jaccard_Sidewalk_without_trees_mean, aes(Axis.1.mean,Axis.2.mean, fill=city, shape = city)) +
  geom_point(size=5, alpha=0.7)+
  geom_errorbar(aes(ymin=Axis.2.mean-Axis.2.se, ymax=Axis.2.mean+Axis.2.se)) +
  geom_errorbar(aes(xmin=Axis.1.mean-Axis.1.se, xmax=Axis.1.mean+Axis.1.se)) +
  labs(x=paste("PCoA1: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[1] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       y=paste("PCoA2: ", round(ITS_ordin_jac_Sidewalk_without_trees$values$Relative_eig[2] * 100, 2), "%", sep=""),
       title="Jaccard Sidewalk_without_trees") +
  scale_fill_manual(values=c("#88CCEE", "#CC6677", "#DDCC77", "#117733", "#332288","#AA4499","#44AA99", "#999933", "#882255", "#661100", "#6699CC", "#888888")) +
  scale_shape_manual(values=c(21,22,23,24,25)) +
  guides(fill=guide_legend(override.aes=list(shape=21)))+
  mytheme

pcoa_bray_all_Sidewalk_without_trees_mean
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/PCOA_Sidewalk_without_trees_error_bars.png",width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)

## And statistics
# Calculate jaccard-Curtis distance matrix
jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_without_trees <- phyloseq::distance(phy_Sidewalk_without_trees, method = "jaccard")

# PERMANOVA for individual variables
adonis_city_Sidewalk_without_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_without_trees ~ city, data= meta_Sidewalk_without_trees)
print(adonis_city_Sidewalk_without_trees)
adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_without_trees <- adonis2(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_without_trees ~ streetname, data= meta_Sidewalk_without_trees)
print(adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_without_trees)


###Permanova assumes equal dispersion amongst groups
#beta_disp <- betadisper(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_without_trees, meta$Group)
#anova(beta_disp)  # Tests if dispersion is significantly different
#permutest(beta_disp)  # Permutation test for dispersion

PairPermITS_Sidewalk_without_trees <- pairwise.perm.manova(jaccard_dist_Sidewalk_without_trees, meta_Sidewalk_without_trees$city, test="Pillai", nperm = 999, p.method = "fdr", F = TRUE, R2 = TRUE)
PairPermITS_Sidewalk_without_trees
PairPermITS_Sidewalk_without_trees$F.value


## For pairwase manova:
# higher F-value means that between-group variation is larger relative to the within group variation
# This generally means a stronger effect of the grouping variable
# Display results


# Flatten and bind rows
combined_results_Sidewalk_without_trees <- bind_rows(data:image/png;base64,iVBORw0KGgoAAAANSUhEUgAAABIAAAASCAYAAABWzo5XAAAAbElEQVR4Xs2RQQrAMAgEfZgf7W9LAguybljJpR3wEse5JOL3ZObDb4x1loDhHbBOFU6i2Ddnw2KNiXcdAXygJlwE8OFVBHDgKrLgSInN4WMe9iXiqIVsTMjH7z/GhNTEibOxQswcYIWYOR/zAjBJfiXh3jZ6AAAAAElFTkSuQmCC
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_topology", adonis_city_Sidewalk_without_trees),
  data.frame(Test = "Pairwise_permanova_topology", PairPermITS_Sidewalk_without_trees$p.value),
  data.frame(Test = "F_value_topology", PairPermITS_Sidewalk_without_trees$F.value),
  data.frame(Test = "Adonis_streetname", adonis_streetname_Sidewalk_without_trees),
  .id = "Source"
)
write.csv(combined_results_Sidewalk_without_trees, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/pcoa/statistical_summary_Sidewalk_without_trees.csv")

rm(list=ls(pattern="Sidewalk_without_trees"))

```

## RC-BRAY

```{r}
library(iCAMP)
phy_rare <- physeq_filtered_16s

set.seed(123)
ASV_com_bact <- t(as.data.frame(phy_rare@otu_table))
metacommunity_df <- data.frame(metacommunity = phy_rare@sam_data$topology)
rownames(metacommunity_df) <- rownames(phy_rare@sam_data)
rc_bray_bact <- RC.cm(com = ASV_com_bact, rand=999, meta.group = metacommunity_df)

hist(rc_bray_bact$index, breaks = 50, main = "RC Bray Distribution", xlab = "RC Bray")
table(rc_bray_bact$sig.index) / length(rc_bray_bact$sig.index)
save.image("./R-scripts/Bacteria_RCBRAY_cd.Rdata")

```

## Functional analysis

### 16s

```{r}
library(microeco)
library(file2meco)
library(vegan)
library(microbiome)

# Loading the FAPROTAX database is more complex. To calculate annotation, we first transform the phyloseq to the meco object
Meco_16S_Rare   <- phyloseq2meco(physeq_filtered_16s)
Meco_16S_Func   <- trans_func$new(dataset =Meco_16S_Rare)


# Calculate the FAPROTAX 1/0 matrix and extraction 
Meco_16S_Func$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16S         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Func$res_spe_func)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) == 0)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) != 0)
sum(rowSums(Func16S, na.rm = F) != 0)/length(rownames(Func16S))
## 26688 ASV are annotated which is 17%

# Note that I will continue with this microeco package, as translating this back into phyloseq is not ideal
```

#### Bar Graph/Heat Maps

```{r}
# Calculating percentage coverage and making a heatmap
Meco_16S_Func$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = TRUE, dec = 8)
Func16SPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Func$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating heatmap x axis order from Ordination (same as phyloseq heatmap)
## extracting ordination coordinates and calculating radial theta
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta_group      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, group = "group", measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16S        <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

# Creating heatmap
Meco_16S_Func$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16S)+
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#FF0000", mid ="#CCFFFF", high = "#001A1A" ,midpoint = 0.00001, na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity (16S data)")


ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/16S_Func_Heat.png", device = png, width = 2000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_Heat2.png", device = png, width = 6000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
#Note the one sample in the middle that is different is B077; Higher levels of chloroplasts, photoautotrophy and also  human associated: maybe Biofilm? Algae? Moss?

# Merged By topology
Meco_16S_Funcx  <- Meco_16S_Rare$merge_samples("topology")

Meco_16S_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_16S_Funcx)
Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16Sx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
Func16SxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating order from chaos
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest      <- Ord16STest$merge_samples("topology")
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16SGroup   <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

# plot

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16SGroup) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(low = "#FF0000", mid = "#CCFFFF", high = "#001A1A",midpoint = 0.00001, na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by topology (16S data)")

write.csv(Func16SxPerc, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_topology.csv")
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_topology.png", device = png, width = 2000, height = 1200, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)

#Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$group <- paste(Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$city, #Meco_16S_Rare$sample_table$topology, sep = "_")

# Merged By group
Meco_16S_Funcx  <- Meco_16S_Rare$merge_samples("topology")

Meco_16S_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_16S_Funcx)
Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func(prok_database = "FAPROTAX")
Func16Sx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_16S_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
Func16SxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# creating order from chaos
Ord16STest      <- Meco_16S_Rare$clone()
Ord16STest      <- Ord16STest$merge_samples("topology")
Ord16STest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = Ord16STest, measure = "jaccard")
Ord16SBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores<- scores(Ord16SBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
Order16SGroup   <- Ord16STest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(Ord16STest, Ord16SBeta, Scores)

library(viridis)
library(scales)
# plot
Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value <- ifelse(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value==NA,0,Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc_trans$value)

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = Order16SGroup) +
    scale_fill_viridis_d(option = "viridis", na.value = "white")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by city and topology (16S data)")

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                  order_x = Order16SGroup) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
    option = "viridis",
    values = rescale(c(0, 0.002, 0.01, 0.3)),  # more detail in the low range
    na.value = "white",
    direction = -1                             # optional: reverse if needed
  ) +
  labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by city and topology (16S data)")

bold_labels <- function(labels, to_bold) {
  sapply(labels, function(label) {
    if (label %in% to_bold) {
      label <- paste0("*", label)
      bquote(bold(.(label)))
    } else {
      label
    }
  })
}
rows_to_make_bold <- as.list(names(signif_functions))

Meco_16S_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                  order_x = c("sidewalk with trees", "sidewalk without trees", "street with trees", "street without trees", "Tree pit")) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
  option = "viridis",
  values = rescale(c(0, 0.002, 0.01, 0.3)),
  breaks = c(0, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.3),
  labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1),
  na.value = "white",
  direction = -1,
  guide = guide_colorbar(barheight = 40, barwidth = 1)
)+
  scale_y_discrete(labels = bold_labels(colnames(Meco_16S_Funcx$res_spe_func), rows_to_make_bold)) +  
  labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by city and topology (16S data)")




  #write.csv(Func16SxPerc, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_City_and_topology.csv")
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/ASV_16S_Func_city_and_topology_viridis_test.png", device = png, width = 2000, height = 2400, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
```

#### Statistics

```{r}

library(rstatix)
## We make a dataframe with metadata and functional data combined.
func_table_with_meta <- cbind(Func16SPerc, as.data.frame(physeq_filtered_16s@sam_data))
write.csv(func_table_with_meta ,"G://My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/test_functional_table_merged.csv")

# Run Kruskal-Wallis test for each function, based on topology. KW because not all values are normally distributed and we should not combine tests.
kw_results <- apply(func_table_with_meta[1:ncol(Func16SPerc)], 2, function(x) kruskal.test(x ~ func_table_with_meta$topology)$p.value)

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing (Benjamini-Hochberg)
kw_results_adj <- p.adjust(kw_results, method = "BH")

# View top significant functions
signif_functions <- sort(kw_results_adj[kw_results_adj < 0.05])
names(signif_functions)
str(names(signif_functions))
func_table_signif <- func_table[,names(signif_functions)]



# Practise to do all pairwise comps.
# for (i in 1:length(names(func_table_signif))){
#   name <- names(signif_functions)[i]
#   pairwise_result <- pairwise_wilcox_test(
#     data.frame(value = func_table_signif[,i], group = category),
#     value ~ group,
#     p.adjust.method = "BH"
#     )
#   pairwise_result_list[name]<- pairwise_result
# }
#   pairwise_result



func_table_signif_merge <-  merge(func_table_signif,physeq_filtered_16s@sam_data,by="row.names",all.x=TRUE)
rownames(func_table_signif_merge) <- func_table_signif_merge$Row.names
func_table$Row.names <- NULL
#One keeps giving an error, so I decided to just remove it.
#names(signif_functions[56])
results_list <- list()
for (func in colnames(func_table_signif)) {
  print(func)
  # Perform pairwise test
  test <- func_table_signif_merge %>%
    dunn_test(as.formula(paste(func, "~ topology")), p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
    add_significance()
  
  # Add median direction info
  median_vals <- func_table_signif_merge %>%
    group_by(topology) %>%
    summarise(med = median(.data[[func]], na.rm = TRUE))
  
  test <- test %>%
    left_join(median_vals, by = c("group1" = "topology")) %>%
    rename(med1 = med) %>%
    left_join(median_vals, by = c("group2" = "topology")) %>%
    rename(med2 = med) %>%
    mutate(direction = case_when(
      p.adj < 0.05 & med1 > med2 ~ paste(group1, ">", group2),
      p.adj < 0.05 & med1 < med2 ~ paste(group1, "<", group2),
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    )) %>%
    mutate(Function = func)
  
  results_list[[func]] <- test
}

# Combine all test results
all_results <- bind_rows(results_list)

write.csv(all_results, "G://My Drive/CityDeal/16S-Sjoerd/Functional_analysis_ASV/pairwise_comparison_functions_topology.csv")

```

### ITS

```{r}
# Having switched to Microeco for functional trait analysis, most of the ITS process has been commented out.

# # FungalTraits
# # extracting the taxtable from phyloseq (rarefied)
# TAXITS <- as.data.frame(tax_table(ASV_ITS_Rare))
# TAXITS1 <- TAXITS
# 
# # Adding guild annotation
# TAXITS1$Genus <- gsub("g__", "", TAXITS1$Genus)
# TAXITS1$Guild <- as.factor(Fungaltraits$guild_fg[match(TAXITS1$Genus, Fungaltraits$Genus)])
# TAXITS1 <- TAXITS1[,c(8,1,2,3,4,5,6,7)]
# # guild has 36 levels
# 
# #new phyloseq
# TAXITS1 <- tax_table(as.matrix(TAXITS1))
# ASV_ITS_Func1 <- phyloseq(otu_table(ASV_ITS_Rare),TAXITS1,Treetment)
# rm(TAXITS1)

library(file2meco)
library(microeco)
#Microeco. Note that these have a more recent database
Meco_ITS_Rare   <- phyloseq2meco(phy_rare)
Meco_ITS_Func   <- trans_func$new(Meco_ITS_Rare)
Meco_ITS_Func$cal_spe_func(fungi_database = "FungalTraits")
FuncITS         <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Func$res_spe_func)
sum(rowSums(FuncITS, na.rm = F) == 0)
sum(rowSums(FuncITS, na.rm = F) != 0)
sum(rowSums(FuncITS, na.rm = F) != 0)/length(rownames(FuncITS))
# Coverage has improved to 37% (at 14.000 annotations)
```

#### Heath_map

```{r}
# FungalTraits x Microeco
# Calculating percentage coverage and making a heatmap
Meco_ITS_Func$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
FuncITSPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Func$res_spe_func_perc)

## extracting ordination coordinates and calculating radial theta
OrdITSTest      <- Meco_ITS_Rare$clone()
OrdITSTest$cal_betadiv(method = "bray")
OrdITSBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = OrdITSTest, group = "topology", measure = "bray")
OrdITSBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(OrdITSBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
OrderITS        <- OrdITSTest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(OrdITSTest, OrdITSBeta, Scores)

Meco_ITS_Func$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = OrderITS)+
    scale_fill_gradient2(mid = "#FFCC99", high = "#1A0D00", na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity (ITS data)")
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Functional_diversity/ASV_ITS_Func_Heat_test.png", device = png, width = 2000, height = 1500, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
ggsave("ASV_ITS_Func_Heat2.png", device = png, width = 6000, height = 1500, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
# note that B077 on the right 10th percentile has very low fungal species richness and low functional percentages

# Merged By city and topology
Meco_ITS_Funcx  <- Meco_ITS_Rare$merge_samples("topology")
Meco_ITS_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_ITS_Funcx)

Meco_ITS_Funcx$cal_spe_func(fungi_database = "FungalTraits")
FuncITSx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_ITS_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
FuncITSxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# Order in the court
OrdITSTest      <- Meco_ITS_Rare$clone()
OrdITSTest      <- OrdITSTest$merge_samples("topology")
OrdITSTest$cal_betadiv(method = "bray")
OrdITSBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = OrdITSTest, measure = "bray")
OrdITSBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(OrdITSBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
OrderITSGroup   <- OrdITSTest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(OrdITSTest, OrdITSBeta, Scores)

Meco_ITS_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                 order_x = OrderITSGroup) +
    scale_fill_gradient2(mid = "#FFCC99", high = "#1A0D00", na.value = "#FF0000")+
    labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by substrate (ITS data)")


ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Functional_diversity/ASV_ITS_Func_city_and_topology_test.png", device = png, width = 2000, height = 1500, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)


# Merged By topology
Meco_ITS_Funcx  <- Meco_ITS_Rare$merge_samples("topology")
Meco_ITS_Funcx  <- trans_func$new(Meco_ITS_Funcx)

Meco_ITS_Funcx$cal_spe_func(fungi_database = "FungalTraits")
FuncITSx         <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Funcx$res_spe_func)

Meco_ITS_Funcx$cal_spe_func_perc(abundance_weighted = TRUE, perc = FALSE, dec = 8)
FuncITSxPerc     <- as.data.frame(Meco_ITS_Funcx$res_spe_func_perc)

# Order in the court
OrdITSTest      <- Meco_ITS_Rare$clone()
OrdITSTest      <- OrdITSTest$merge_samples("topology")

OrdITSTest$cal_betadiv(method = "jaccard")
OrdITSBeta      <- trans_beta$new(dataset = OrdITSTest, measure = "jaccard")
OrdITSBeta$cal_ordination(method = "PCoA")
Scores          <- scores(OrdITSBeta$res_ordination, choices = c(1,2), display = "sites")
OrderITSGroup   <- OrdITSTest$sample_names()[order(radial_theta(Scores))]

rm(OrdITSTest, OrdITSBeta, Scores)

# Meco_ITS_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
#                                  order_x = OrderITSGroup) +
#     scale_fill_gradient2(mid = "#FFCC99", high = "#1A0D00", na.value = "#FF0010")+
#     labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by substrate (ITS data)")
bold_labels <- function(labels, to_bold) {
  sapply(labels, function(label) {
    if (label %in% to_bold) {
      label <- paste0("*", label)
      bquote(bold(.(label)))
    } else {
      label
    }
  })
}

intersect(colnames(Meco_ITS_Func$res_spe_func), rows_to_make_bold)
rows_to_make_bold <- sub(".*\\|", "", names(signif_functions))
Meco_ITS_Funcx$plot_spe_func_perc(add_facet = TRUE,
                                  order_x = c("Sidewalk_with_trees", "Sidewalk_without_trees", "Street_with_trees", "Street_without_trees", "Treepit")) +
  scale_fill_viridis(
  option = "viridis",
  values = rescale(c(0, 0.1, 0.4, 0.8)),
  #breaks = c(0, 0.005, 0.01, 0.05, 0.1, 0.3),
  labels = scales::label_percent(accuracy = 0.1),
  na.value = "white",
  direction = -1,
  guide = guide_colorbar(barheight = 40, barwidth = 1)
)+
  scale_y_discrete(labels = bold_labels(y_labels, rows_to_make_bold)) +
  labs(title = "Heatmap of functional diversity grouped by topology (ITS data)")

y_labels <- unique(p$data$variable)


ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Functional_diversity/ASV_ITS_Functopology_viridis_Bold.png", device = png, width = 2400, height = 2880, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)



```

#### Statistics

```{r}
# Assuming your function table is `func_table` and metadata is `meta_table`
# Ensure rows in func_table match sample order in meta_table
func_table <- FuncITSPerc[rownames(phy_rare@sam_data), ]
category <- as.factor(phy_rare@sam_data$city)

# Run Kruskal-Wallis test for each function
kw_results <- apply(func_table, 2, function(x) kruskal.test(x ~ category)$p.value)

# Adjust p-values for multiple testing (e.g., Benjamini-Hochberg)
kw_results_adj <- p.adjust(kw_results, method = "BH")

# View top significant functions
signif_functions <- sort(kw_results_adj[kw_results_adj < 0.05])
names(signif_functions)
str(names(signif_functions))
func_table_signif <- func_table[,names(signif_functions)]

library(rstatix)

func_table_signif_merge <-  merge(func_table_signif,phy_rare@sam_data,by="row.names",all.x=TRUE)
rownames(func_table_signif_merge) <- func_table_signif_merge$Row.names
func_table$Row.names <- NULL
names(func_table_signif_merge) <- make.names(names(func_table_signif_merge))
names(func_table_signif) <- make.names(names(func_table_signif))
results_list <- list()
for (func in colnames(func_table_signif)) {
  print(func)
  # Perform pairwise test
  test <- func_table_signif_merge %>%
    dunn_test(as.formula(paste(func, "~ topology")), p.adjust.method = "BH") %>%
    add_significance()
  
  # Add median direction info
  median_vals <- func_table_signif_merge %>%
    group_by(topology) %>%
    summarise(med = median(.data[[func]], na.rm = TRUE))
  
  test <- test %>%
    left_join(median_vals, by = c("group1" = "topology")) %>%
    rename(med1 = med) %>%
    left_join(median_vals, by = c("group2" = "topology")) %>%
    rename(med2 = med) %>%
    mutate(direction = case_when(
      p.adj < 0.05 & med1 > med2 ~ paste(group1, ">", group2),
      p.adj < 0.05 & med1 < med2 ~ paste(group1, "<", group2),
      TRUE ~ "NS"
    )) %>%
    mutate(Function = func)
  
  results_list[[func]] <- test
}

# Combine all test results
all_results <- bind_rows(results_list)

write.csv(all_results, "G://My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Functional_diversity/pairwise_comparison_functions.csv")

```

# Might use

## ICAMP

```{r}
library(iCAMP)
library(NST)
```

```{r}
#Panjis code
wd="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd"
save.wd="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Community_assembly"
nworker=4 # parallell computing-thread number.
memory_use=13 #Total RAM in Gb used.
rand.time=100 # randomization time for null model analysis (ini 100 supaya cepet aja, nanti disesuaikan biasanya 9999 untuk betanmtd dan 999 untuk betanti)
prefix="Citydeal_comm_assembly_ITS" # prefix in the output filenames

comm=t(data.frame(otu_table(phy_rare))) #ini otu table nya
group=data.frame(sample_data(phy_rare)) #ini metadatanya
tree=ape::read.tree("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/ITS_tree_citydeal_genus.nwk")
tree_taxa <- tree$tip.label
tree$tip.label <- gsub('\"\"\"', '', tree_taxa)

samp.ck=NST::match.name(rn.list=list(comm=comm,group=group)) #ngecek kesesuaian nama sampel antara data di metadata dan data di OTU table
comm=samp.ck$comm #karena datanya sudah sesuai, maka dibuat baru
comm=comm[,colSums(comm)>0,drop=FALSE]
group=samp.ck$group

tax.ck=NST::match.name(cn.list = list(comm=comm), tree.list = list(tree=tree)) #kesesuaian nama OTU dan anama yang di phylogenetic tree
comm=tax.ck$comm
tree=tax.ck$tree

# 2. Calculate bNTI and RCI ----
## 2.1. Using iCAMP - Method 1 ----

set.seed(1234)
qpen <- qpen(comm = comm,
             pd = NULL,
             pd.big.wd = NULL,
             pd.big.spname = NULL,
             tree = tree,
             bNTI = NULL,
             RC = NULL,
             ab.weight = TRUE,
             meta.ab = NULL,
             exclude.conspecifics = FALSE,
             rand.time = rand.time,
             sig.bNTI = 2,
             sig.rc = 0.95,
             nworker = nworker,
             memory.G = memory_use,
             project = NA,
             wd = save.wd,
             output.detail = FALSE,
             save.bNTIRC = FALSE,
             taxo.metric = "bray",
             transform.method = NULL,
             logbase = 2,
             dirichlet = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# version 2020.8.23
# version 2020.9.21, add classification information
# version 2021.1.7, add (step 15) icamp.cate to summarize for different categories of taxa, e.g. core versus rare taxa.

t0=Sys.time() # to calculate time cost

# 1 # set folder paths and file names, please change according to the folder paths and file names in your computer.
# the folder saving the input files
wd="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd"

# the OTU table file (Tab delimited txt file)
com.file="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/meta_11912_ITS_alpha.csv"

# the phylogenetic tree file
tree.file="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/ITS_tree_citydeal-used.nwk"

# the classification (taxonomy) information
clas.file="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS_ASV_Tables/Taxonomy_table_Citydeal.txt"

# the treatment informaiton table
treat.file="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/meta_11912_ITS_alpha.csv"

# the environmental varialbes
#env.file="environment.txt"
# if you do not have env file or the env may not represent niche, skip step 7 and 8, but check the alternative way to determine binning setting, e.g. bin.size.limit.

# the folder to save the output. please change to a new folder even if you are just testing the example data.
save.wd="G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Community_assembly"
if(!dir.exists(save.wd)){dir.create(save.wd)}

# 2 # key parameter setting
prefix="Citydeal_comm_assembly_ITS"  # prefix of the output file names. usually use a project ID.
rand.time=1000  # randomization time, 1000 is usually enough. For example test, you may set as 100 or less to save time.
nworker=4 # nworker is thread number for parallel computing, which depends on the CPU core number of your computer.
memory.G=10 # to set the memory size as you need (but should be less than the available space in your hard disk), so that calculation of large tree will not be limited by physical memory. unit is Gb.

# 3 # load R packages and data
library(iCAMP)
library(ape)
setwd(wd)
comm=t(read.table(com.file, header = TRUE, sep = ",", row.names = 1,
                  as.is = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, comment.char = "",
                  check.names = FALSE))
tree=read.tree(file = tree.file)
clas=read.table(clas.file, header = TRUE, sep = ",", row.names = 1,
                as.is = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, comment.char = "",
                check.names = FALSE)

treat=read.table(treat.file, header = TRUE, sep = ",", row.names = 1,
                 as.is = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, comment.char = "",
                 check.names = FALSE)

#env=read.table(env.file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", row.names = 1,
 #              as.is = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, comment.char = "",
  #             check.names = FALSE) # skip this if you do not have env.file

# 4 # match sample IDs in OTU table and treatment information table
sampid.check=match.name(rn.list=list(comm=comm,treat=treat))
# sampid.check=match.name(rn.list=list(comm=comm,treat=treat)) # if you do not have env.file
# for the example data, the output should be "All match very well".
# for your data files, if you have not matched their IDs, the unmatched samples will be removed.
treat=sampid.check$treat
comm=sampid.check$comm
comm=comm[,colSums(comm)>0,drop=FALSE] # if some unmatched samples were removed, some OTUs may become ghosts, then you may use this line to remove them if necessary.
env=sampid.check$env # skip this if you do not have env.file

# 5 # match OTU IDs in OTU table and tree file
spid.check=match.name(cn.list=list(comm=comm),rn.list=list(clas=clas),tree.list=list(tree=tree))
# for the example data, the output should be "All match very well".
# for your data files, if you have not matched the IDs before, the unmatched OTUs will be removed.
comm=spid.check$comm
clas=spid.check$clas
tree=spid.check$tree

# 6 # calculate pairwise phylogenetic distance matrix.
# since microbial community data usually has a large number of species (OTUs or ASVs), we use "big.matrix" in R package "bigmemory" to handle the large phylogenetic distance matrix. 
setwd(save.wd)
if(!file.exists("pd.desc")) 
{
  pd.big=iCAMP::pdist.big(tree = tree, wd=save.wd, nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G)
  # output files:
  # path.rda: a R object to list all the nodes and  edge lengthes from root to every tip. saved in R data format. an intermediate output when claculating phylogenetic distance matrix.
  # pd.bin: BIN file (backingfile) generated by function big.matrix in R package bigmemory. This is the big matrix storing pairwise phylogenetic distance values. By using this bigmemory format file, we will not need memory but hard disk when calling big matrix for calculation.
  # pd.desc: the DESC file (descriptorfile) to hold the backingfile (pd.bin) description.
  # pd.taxon.name.csv: comma delimited csv file storing the IDs of tree tips (OTUs), serving as the row/column names of the big phylogenetic distance matrix.
}else{
  # if you already calculated the phylogenetic distance matrix in a previous run
  pd.big=list()
  pd.big$tip.label=read.csv(paste0(save.wd,"/pd.taxon.name.csv"),row.names = 1,stringsAsFactors = FALSE)[,1]
  pd.big$pd.wd=save.wd
  pd.big$pd.file="pd.desc"
  pd.big$pd.name.file="pd.taxon.name.csv"
}

####################
# you may skip step 7-8, if the "alternative way" based on stochasticity is applicable, as mentioned in the method part of iCAMP paper (Ning et al 2020 Nature Communications).
# 7 # assess niche preference difference between species
# env is required for this step.
# since microbial community data usually has a large number of species (OTUs or ASVs), we use "big.matrix" in R package "bigmemory" to handle the large niche difference matrix. 
setwd(save.wd)
niche.dif=iCAMP::dniche(env = env,comm = comm,method = "niche.value",
                        nworker = nworker,out.dist=FALSE,bigmemo=TRUE,
                        nd.wd=save.wd)

# 8 # within-bin phylogenetic signal assessment.
# For real data, you may try several different settings of binning, and choose the one leading to the best within-bin phylogenetic signal.
# env is required for this step.
# 8.1 # try phylogenetic binning using current setttings.
ds = 0.2 # setting can be changed to explore the best choice
bin.size.limit = 5 # setting can be changed to explore the best choice. # here set as 5 just for the small example dataset. For real data, usually try 12 to 48.
phylobin=taxa.binphy.big(tree = tree, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file,pd.spname = pd.big$tip.label,
                         pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, ds = ds, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit,
                         nworker = nworker)
# 8.2 # test within-bin phylogenetic signal.
sp.bin=phylobin$sp.bin[,3,drop=FALSE]
sp.ra=colMeans(comm/rowSums(comm))
abcut=3 # you may remove some species, if they are too rare to perform reliable correlation test.
commc=comm[,colSums(comm)>=abcut,drop=FALSE]
dim(commc)
spname.use=colnames(commc)
binps=iCAMP::ps.bin(sp.bin = sp.bin,sp.ra = sp.ra,spname.use = spname.use,
                    pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname = pd.big$tip.label, pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd,
                    nd.list = niche.dif$nd,nd.spname = niche.dif$names,ndbig.wd = niche.dif$nd.wd,
                    cor.method = "pearson",r.cut = 0.1, p.cut = 0.05, min.spn = 5)
if(file.exists(paste0(prefix,".PhyloSignalSummary.csv"))){appendy=TRUE;col.namesy=FALSE}else{appendy=FALSE;col.namesy=TRUE}
write.table(data.frame(ds=ds,n.min=bin.size.limit,binps$Index),file = paste0(prefix,".PhyloSignalSummary.csv"),
            append = appendy, quote=FALSE, sep=",", row.names = FALSE,col.names = col.namesy)
if(file.exists(paste0(prefix,".PhyloSignalDetail.csv"))){appendy2=TRUE;col.namesy2=FALSE}else{appendy2=FALSE;col.namesy2=TRUE}
write.table(data.frame(ds=ds,n.min=bin.size.limit,binID=rownames(binps$detail),binps$detail),file = paste0(prefix,".PhyloSignalDetail.csv"),
            append = appendy2, quote = FALSE, sep = ",", row.names = FALSE, col.names = col.namesy2)
# since this example small data is randomly generated, the correlation should be very weak.
# usually, you are looking for a binning setting lead to higher RAsig.abj (relative abundance of bins with significant phylogenetic signal) and relative high meanR (mean correlation coefficient across bins).
# see help document of the function "ps.bin" for the meaning of output.

####################
# 9 # iCAMP analysis
# 9.1 # without omitting small bins.
# commonly use # set sig.index as Confidence instead of SES.RC (betaNRI/NTI + RCbray)
bin.size.limit = 5 # For real data, usually use a proper number according to phylogenetic signal test or try some settings then choose the reasonable stochasticity level. our experience is 12, or 24, or 48. but for this example dataset which is too small, have to use 5.
sig.index="Confidence" # see other options in help document of icamp.big.
icres=iCAMP::icamp.big(comm=comm, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label,
                       pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, rand = rand.time, tree=tree,
                       prefix = prefix, ds = 0.2, pd.cut = NA, sp.check = TRUE,
                       phylo.rand.scale = "within.bin", taxa.rand.scale = "across.all",
                       phylo.metric = "bMPD", sig.index=sig.index, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit, 
                       nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G, rtree.save = FALSE, detail.save = TRUE, 
                       qp.save = FALSE, detail.null = FALSE, ignore.zero = TRUE, output.wd = save.wd, 
                       correct.special = TRUE, unit.sum = rowSums(comm), special.method = "depend",
                       ses.cut = 1.96, rc.cut = 0.95, conf.cut=0.975, omit.option = "no",meta.ab = NULL)
# there are quite a few parameters in this function, please check the help document of "icamp.big".
# output files:
# Test.iCAMP.detail.rda: the object "icres" saved in R data format. it is a list object. The first element bNRIiRCa is the result of relative importance of each assembly process in each pairwise comparison (each turnover). The second element "detail" including binning information (named taxabin), phylogenetic and taxonomic metrics results in each bin (named like bNRIi, RCa, etc.), relative abundance of each bin (bin.weight), relative importance of each process in each turnover between communities (processes), input settings (setting), and input community data matrix (comm). See help document of the function icamp.big for more details.

############################
# 9.2 to 9.4 are some optional special settings you may explore.
# 9.2 # explore different ways for null model significance test.
# 9.2.1 # set detail.null=TRUE, output all null values, to facilitate normality test and switch between different options
detail.null=TRUE
bin.size.limit = 5 
sig.index="SES.RC" # this is traditional way, with assumption that null values of phylogenetic metrics follow normal distribution. 
prefixb="TestB"

icres2=iCAMP::icamp.big(comm=comm, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label,
                       pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, rand = rand.time, tree=tree,
                       prefix = prefixb, ds = 0.2, pd.cut = NA, sp.check = TRUE,
                       phylo.rand.scale = "within.bin", taxa.rand.scale = "across.all",
                       phylo.metric = "bMPD", sig.index=sig.index, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit, 
                       nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G, rtree.save = FALSE, detail.save = TRUE, 
                       qp.save = FALSE, detail.null = detail.null, ignore.zero = TRUE, output.wd = save.wd, 
                       correct.special = TRUE, unit.sum = rowSums(comm), special.method = "depend",
                       ses.cut = 1.96, rc.cut = 0.95, conf.cut=0.975, omit.option = "no",meta.ab = NULL)
# 9.2.2 # normality test
nntest=iCAMP::null.norm(icamp.output=icres2, p.norm.cut=0.05, detail.out=FALSE)
# output shows non-normal distribution ratio in each bin, i.e. the proportion of turnovers which have null values significantly deviated from normal distribution.
# if some ratio values are very high, may need to change to use "Confidence" as sig.index.

# 9.2.3 # change sig.index to "Confidence".
icres3=iCAMP::change.sigindex(icamp.output = icres2, sig.index = "Confidence", detail.save = TRUE, detail.null = FALSE, conf.cut = 0.975)
head(icres3$CbMPDiCBraya)

# 9.2.4 # change sig.index to "RC" for both phylogenetic and taxonomic metrics.
icres4=iCAMP::change.sigindex(icamp.output = icres2, sig.index = "RC", detail.save = TRUE, detail.null = FALSE, rc.cut = 0.95)
head(icres4$RCbMPDiRCbraya)

# 9.2.5 # the function can also change the significance threshold.
icres5=iCAMP::change.sigindex(icamp.output = icres2, sig.index = "SES.RC", detail.save = TRUE, detail.null = FALSE, ses.cut = 1.64, rc.cut = 0.9)
head(icres5$bNRIiRCbraya)

# 9.3 # you may specify the relative abundance of each species in the regional pool, if it is not the same as the average relative abundance from the "comm" you input.
meta.ab=rep(1,ncol(comm)) # here i assume all the species actuall have the same relative abundance in the regional pool.
prefix2=paste0(prefix,".MetaCrct")
sig.index="Confidence" # see other options in help document of icamp.big.
icres.meta=iCAMP::icamp.big(comm=comm, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label,
                       pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, rand = rand.time, tree=tree,
                       prefix = prefix2, ds = 0.2, pd.cut = NA, sp.check = TRUE,
                       phylo.rand.scale = "within.bin", taxa.rand.scale = "across.all",
                       phylo.metric = "bMPD", sig.index=sig.index, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit, 
                       nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G, rtree.save = FALSE, detail.save = TRUE, 
                       qp.save = FALSE, detail.null = FALSE, ignore.zero = TRUE, output.wd = save.wd, 
                       correct.special = TRUE, unit.sum = rowSums(comm), special.method = "depend",
                       ses.cut = 1.96, rc.cut = 0.95, conf.cut=0.975, omit.option = "no",meta.ab=meta.ab)

# 9.4 # consider to omit small bins
# 9.4.1 # if you would like to omit small bins rather than merging them to nearest relatives, set omit.option as "test" to check what will be omitted.
omit.option = "test"
icres.omit=iCAMP::icamp.big(comm=comm, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label,
                            pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, rand = rand.time, tree=tree,
                            prefix = prefix, ds = 0.2, pd.cut = NA, sp.check = TRUE,
                            phylo.rand.scale = "within.bin", taxa.rand.scale = "across.all",
                            phylo.metric = "bMPD", sig.index=sig.index, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit, 
                            nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G, rtree.save = FALSE, detail.save = TRUE, 
                            qp.save = FALSE, detail.null=FALSE, ignore.zero = TRUE, output.wd = save.wd, 
                            correct.special = TRUE, unit.sum = rowSums(comm), special.method = "depend",
                            ses.cut = 1.96, rc.cut = 0.95, conf.cut=0.975, omit.option = omit.option)
# "test" will return a detailed table of omitted species.

# 9.4.2 # then set it as "omit" to omit the small bins.
omit.option = "omit"
icres.omit2=iCAMP::icamp.big(comm=comm, pd.desc = pd.big$pd.file, pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label,
                            pd.wd = pd.big$pd.wd, rand = rand.time, tree=tree,
                            prefix = prefix, ds = 0.2, pd.cut = NA, sp.check = TRUE,
                            phylo.rand.scale = "within.bin", taxa.rand.scale = "across.all",
                            phylo.metric = "bMPD", sig.index=sig.index, bin.size.limit = bin.size.limit, 
                            nworker = nworker, memory.G = memory.G, rtree.save = FALSE, detail.save = TRUE, 
                            qp.save = FALSE, detail.null=FALSE, ignore.zero = TRUE, output.wd = save.wd, 
                            correct.special = TRUE, unit.sum = rowSums(comm), special.method = "depend",
                            ses.cut = 1.96, rc.cut = 0.95, conf.cut=0.975, omit.option = omit.option)
# In this simple example, since all bins are small, "omit" should return an error. In real data, this will go ahead to do iCAMP analysis with the strict bins which are big enough (>bin.size.limit).
###############################


# 10 # iCAMP bin level statistics
icbin=icamp.bins(icamp.detail = icres$detail,treat = treat,
                 clas=clas,silent=FALSE, boot = TRUE,
                 rand.time = rand.time,between.group = TRUE)
save(icbin,file = paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.Summary.rda")) # just to archive the result. rda file is automatically compressed, and easy to load into R.
write.csv(icbin$Pt,file = paste0(prefix,".ProcessImportance_EachGroup.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(icbin$Ptk,file = paste0(prefix,".ProcessImportance_EachBin_EachGroup.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(icbin$Ptuv,file = paste0(prefix,".ProcessImportance_EachTurnover.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(icbin$BPtk,file = paste0(prefix,".BinContributeToProcess_EachGroup.csv"),row.names = FALSE)

write.csv(data.frame(ID=rownames(icbin$Class.Bin),icbin$Class.Bin,stringsAsFactors = FALSE),
          file = paste0(prefix,".Taxon_Bin.csv"),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(icbin$Bin.TopClass,file = paste0(prefix,".Bin_TopTaxon.csv"),row.names = FALSE)

# output files:
# Test.iCAMP.Summary.rda: the object "icbin" saved in R data format. see help document of the function icamp.bins for description of each element in the object.
# Test.ProcessImportance_EachGroup.csv: Relative importance of each process in governing the turnovers in a group of samples.
# Test.ProcessImportance_EachBin_EachGroup.csv: Relative importance of each process in governing the turnovers of each bin among a group of samples.
# Test.ProcessImportance_EachTurnover.csv: Relative importance of each process in governing the turnovers between each pair of communities (samples).
# Test.BinContributeToProcess_EachGroup.csv: Bin contribution to each process, measuring the contribution of each bin to the relative importance of each process in the assembly of a group of communities.
# Test.Taxon_Bin.csv: a matrix showing the bin ID and classification information for each taxon.
# Test.Bin_TopTaxon.csv: a matrix showing the bin relative abundance; the top taxon ID, percentage in bin, and classification; the most abundant name at each phylogeny level in the bin.

# 11 # Bootstrapping test
# please specify which column in the treatment information table.
i=1
treat.use=treat[,i,drop=FALSE]
icamp.result=icres$CbMPDiCBraya
icboot=iCAMP::icamp.boot(icamp.result = icamp.result,treat = treat.use,rand.time = rand.time,
                         compare = TRUE,silent = FALSE,between.group = TRUE,ST.estimation = TRUE)
save(icboot,file=paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.Boot.",colnames(treat)[i],".rda"))
write.csv(icboot$summary,file = paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.BootSummary.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"),row.names = FALSE)
write.csv(icboot$compare,file = paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.Compare.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"),row.names = FALSE)

# output files:
# Test.iCAMP.Boot.Management.rda: the object "icboot" saved in R data format. see help document of the function icamp.boot for description of each element in the object.
# Test.BootSummary.Management.csv: a table to summarize bootstrapping results. see help document of the function icamp.boot for description of the output element "summary".
# Test.Compare.Management.csv: a table to summarize comparison index, effect size, and significance between each two groups. see help document of the function icamp.boot for description of the output element "compare".

# 12 # Other approach: QPEN (quantifying community assembly processes based on entire-community null model analysis)
qpout=iCAMP::qpen(comm=comm,pd=pd.big$pd.file,pd.big.wd=pd.big$pd.wd,
                  pd.big.spname=pd.big$tip.label,ab.weight=TRUE,
                  rand.time=rand.time, nworker=nworker,project=prefix,
                  wd=save.wd, save.bNTIRC=TRUE)

# 13 # Other approach: Neutral taxa percentage
snmout=iCAMP::snm.comm(comm = comm, treat = treat, 
                       rand=rand.time, alpha=0.05)
write.csv(snmout$stats,file = paste0(prefix,".NeutralModel.Stats.csv"))
write.csv(snmout$ratio.summary,file = paste0(prefix,".NeutralModel.TypeRatio.csv"))

# 14 # Other approach: tNST and pNST (taxonomic and phylogenetic normalized stochasticity ratio)
# need to install package NST if not yet
if(!("NST" %in% installed.packages()[,"Package"])){install.packages("NST")}
library(NST)
i=1
treat.use=treat[,i,drop=FALSE]

# 14.1a # tNST
tnstout=NST::tNST(comm=comm, group=treat.use, dist.method="bray", 
                  abundance.weighted=TRUE, rand=rand.time,  
                  nworker=nworker, null.model="PF", output.rand = TRUE,
                  SES = TRUE, RC = TRUE)
write.csv(tnstout$index.grp,file = paste0(prefix,".tNST.summary.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))
write.csv(tnstout$index.pair.grp,file = paste0(prefix,".tNST.pairwise.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))

# 14.1b # bootstrapping test for tNST
tnst.bt=NST::nst.boot(nst.result=tnstout, group=treat.use,
                      rand=rand.time, nworker=nworker)
write.csv(tnst.bt$NST.summary,file = paste0(prefix,".tNST.bootstr.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))
write.csv(tnst.bt$NST.compare,file = paste0(prefix,".tNST.compare.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))

# 14.2a # pNST
pnstout=NST::pNST(comm=comm, pd.desc=pd.big$pd.file, pd.wd=pd.big$pd.wd, 
                  pd.spname=pd.big$tip.label, group=treat.use, abundance.weighted=TRUE,
                  rand=rand.time, phylo.shuffle=TRUE, nworker=nworker,
                  output.rand = TRUE, SES=FALSE, RC=FALSE)
write.csv(pnstout$index.grp,file = paste0(prefix,".pNST.summary.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))
write.csv(pnstout$index.pair.grp,file = paste0(prefix,".pNST.pairwise.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))

pnst.bt=NST::nst.boot(nst.result=pnstout, group=treat.use,
                      rand=rand.time, nworker=nworker)
write.csv(pnst.bt$NST.summary,file = paste0(prefix,".pNST.bootstr.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))
write.csv(pnst.bt$NST.compare,file = paste0(prefix,".pNST.compare.",colnames(treat)[i],".csv"))

# 15 # summarize core, rare, and other taxa
# 15.1 # define the types of different taxa in category.txt
setwd(wd)
cate.file="category.txt"
cate=read.table(cate.file, header = TRUE, sep = "\t", row.names = 1,
                as.is = TRUE, stringsAsFactors = FALSE, comment.char = "",
                check.names = FALSE)
cate=cate[which(rownames(cate) %in% colnames(comm)),,drop=FALSE] # remove unmatched taxa.
setwd(save.wd)

# 15.2
iccate=icamp.cate(icamp.bins.result = icbin,comm = comm,cate = cate,
                  treat = treat, silent = FALSE,between.group = TRUE)
write.csv(iccate$Ptuvx,file = paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.Process_EachTurnover_EachCategory.csv"))
write.csv(iccate$Ptx,file = paste0(prefix,".iCAMP.Process_EachGroup_EachCategory.csv"))

(t=format(Sys.time()-t0)) # to calculate time cost
# End #
```

read ASV table

```{r}

ITS_ASV <- read.csv("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/ASV_ITS_rarefied11912.csv", row.names = 1)
ITS_ASV <- t(ITS_ASV)
```

And now do the analysis

```{r}
qpen(comm = ITS_ASV, tree = citydeal_tree, project = "citydeal_ITS_communityAssembly",
     wd = "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912", save.bNTIRC = T,
     taxo.metric = "bray")
```

## Differential

#### ITS

##### DESeq

functions

```{r}
CoreBiomeDESeq <- function(x, Contrastname, Contrastlist, a){
    CL <- length(Contrastlist)  # number of granulates
    Corelist <- row.names(x)    # Start out with the whole list of ASVs
    for(i in 1:(CL-1)){         # per substrate
        for(ii in 1:(CL-i)){    # all pairings
            TempDESeqResults <- results(x, contrast = c(Contrastname, Contrastlist[[i]], Contrastlist[[(i+ii)]]), alpha = a) # get results of deseq
            Sublist <- row.names(subset(TempDESeqResults, TempDESeqResults$padj>a))  # get all ASVs in this pairing where Padj is not sig
            Corelist <- Corelist[(Corelist %in% Sublist)] # prune the working list down to only the ASVs that are not sig, for every pairing
        }
    }
    print(Corelist)
}

# Note: This function uses the adjusted p values for a pairwise comparison. Technically, by conducting 78 pairwise comparisons, the p values should be adjusted after the whole thing is finished. This would require making a much larger function which maintains a running data frame with all p values, adjust them all together after the pairwise comparisons, and then extracts the target ASVs. For time reasons, I have been unable to do that and I have left this as an excercise for the reader.
# Also: If it is necessary to make a new function, it may be possible to craft a function that incorporates all variants of this function I have used in this script: per DA analysis: one corelist, one DA list and one PureDA list (not as essential as it is likely to be empty)



# Determining a differential biome that is always differential
# Only difference in this function is the <a in line 8
DiffBiomeDESeq <- function(x, Contrastname, Contrastlist, a){
    CL <- length(Contrastlist)
    Corelist <- row.names(x)
    for(i in 1:(CL-1)){
        for(ii in 1:(CL-i)){
            TempDESeqResults <- results(x, contrast = c(Contrastname, Contrastlist[[i]], Contrastlist[[(i+ii)]]), alpha = a)
            Sublist <- row.names(subset(TempDESeqResults, TempDESeqResults$padj<a))
            Corelist <- Corelist[(Corelist %in% Sublist)]
        }
    }
    print(Corelist)
}


# None are differentially abundant in all pairwise comparisons

# Determining the ASVs that are differentially abundant
DABiomeDESeq <- function(x, Contrastname, Contrastlist, a){
    CL <- length(Contrastlist)
    Corelist <- character()
    for(i in 1:(CL-1)){
        for(ii in 1:(CL-i)){
            TempDESeqResults <- results(x, contrast = c(Contrastname, Contrastlist[[i]], Contrastlist[[(i+ii)]]), alpha = a)
            Sublist <- row.names(subset(TempDESeqResults, TempDESeqResults$padj<a))
            Corelist <- unique(c(Sublist, Corelist))
        }
    }
    print(sort(Corelist, decreasing = FALSE))
}
```

and run it

```{r}
topology_list <- levels(phy_rare@sam_data$topology)
ASV_ITS_Deseq <- phyloseq_to_deseq2(phy_rare, ~topology)
ASV_ITS_Deseq <- DESeq(ASV_ITS_Deseq, test="Wald", fitType="parametric")


# Running the function for Core Biome
Corelist_ITS_DESeq <- CoreBiomeDESeq(ASV_ITS_Deseq, Contrastname = "topology", Contrastlist = topology_list, a = 0.05)
# Takes about 5 mins

# Determining the ASVs that are differentially abundant
DAList_ITS_DESeq <- DABiomeDESeq(phy_rare, Contrastname = "topology", Contrastlist = levels(phy_rare@sam_data$topology), a = 0.05)
# again 5 mins
```

## ALDEX2

```{r}
BiocManager::install("microbiomeMarker")
```

```{r}
library("microbiomeMarker"n)
```

##### ADAPT

The function

```{r}
CoreBiomeADAPT <- function(x, Contrastname, Contrastlist, a){
    Corelist <- taxa_names(x)
    for(i in 1:length(Contrastlist)){
        tempAdapt <- adapt(input_data = x, 
                           cond.var = Contrastname, 
                           base.cond = Contrastlist[[i]], 
                           prev.filter = 0.01)
        tempdf <- as.data.frame(summary(tempAdapt, select = "all"))
        Sublist <- row.names(subset(tempdf, tempdf$adjusted_pval>a))
        Corelist <- Corelist[(Corelist %in% Sublist)]
    }
    print(Corelist)
}

DABiomeADAPT <- function(x, Contrastname, Contrastlist, a){
    Corelist <- character()
    for(i in 1:length(Contrastlist)){
        tempAdapt <- adapt(input_data = x, 
                           cond.var = Contrastname, 
                           base.cond = Contrastlist[[i]], 
                           prev.filter = 0.01)
        tempdf <- as.data.frame(summary(tempAdapt, select = "all"))
        Sublist <- row.names(subset(tempdf, tempdf$adjusted_pval<a))
        Corelist <- unique(c(Sublist, Corelist))
    }
    print(sort(Corelist, decreasing = FALSE))
}
```

And the calculation

```{r}

#BiocManager::install("ADAPT")
#library("ADAPT")
Corelist_ITS_ADAPT <- CoreBiomeADAPT(phy_rare, Contrastname = "topology", Contrastlist = levels(phy_rare@sam_data$topology), a = 0.05)

DAList_ITS_ADAPT <- DABiomeADAPT(phy_rare, Contrastname = "topology", Contrastlist = levels(phy_rare@sam_data$topology), a = 0.05)
```

##### Consensus

```{r}
# comparing Lists of DESeq and ADAPT
Corelist_ITS_Consensus <- Corelist_ITS_DESeq[(Corelist_ITS_DESeq %in% Corelist_ITS_ADAPT)]
# DESeq and ADAPT agree on 484 ASV that are non differential between all pairings.
# Note that ADAPT appears much more strict on what counts as differential, given the much larger list (7276 vs 688)

DAlist_ITS_Consensus <- DAList_ITS_ADAPT[(DAList_ITS_ADAPT %in% DAList_ITS_DESeq)]
# Consensus between the 2 sets on 187 ASVs

# Making a barplot of the Core (proportional. Aside from the cumulative changes over many asvs, we will still have the problem that we removed 4 samples from the ITS set, making raw abundance look less even than the numerical tests imply)

Core_ITS <- prune_taxa(Corelist_ITS_Consensus, ASV_ITS_Rare)
Core_ITS <- merge_samples(Core_ITS, "Productcode")
Core_ITS <- transform_sample_counts(Core_ITS, function(x) x/sum(x)*100)
Core_ITS <- tax_glom(Core_ITS, taxrank = "Phylum", NArm = FALSE)
plot_bar(Core_ITS, fill = "Phylum")
# Despite the proportional arrangement, the cumulative changes may have to much of an impact still. There are also a much higher proportion of NA and incertis than with the bacterial set. This is possibly a result of annotation bias.

# Making 6 heatmaps of the variable group at different glom levels Note: the ordering of the taxa is by phylum, disrupting the ordination order. The last plot is only ordered by ordination.

Variable_ITS <- prune_taxa(DAlist_ITS_Consensus, ASV_ITS_Rare)
Variable_ITS <- merge_samples(Variable_ITS, "Productcode")
ASV <- colnames(otu_table(Variable_ITS))
tax_table(Variable_ITS) <- cbind(tax_table(Variable_ITS), ASV)
```
