```{r}
rm(ASV)

## extracting ordination coordinates and calculating radial theta
ord_ITSrPCA     <- ordinate(Variable_ITS, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard")
Scoresord       <- ord_ITSrPCA$vectors
OrderITS        <- sample_names(Variable_ITS)[order(radial_theta(Scoresord))]

rm(ord_ITSrPCA, Scoresord)


plot_heatmap(Variable_ITS, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray", taxa.label = "ASV", sample.order = OrderITS, taxa.order = "Phylum", max.label = 1200, low = "#331900", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000")

Variable_ITSG <- tax_glom(Variable_ITS, taxrank = "Genus", NArm = FALSE)
plot_heatmap(Variable_ITSG, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray", taxa.label = "Genus", sample.order = OrderITS, taxa.order = "Phylum", max.label = 1200, low = "#331900", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000")

Variable_ITSF <- tax_glom(Variable_ITS, taxrank = "Family", NArm = FALSE)
plot_heatmap(Variable_ITSF, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray", taxa.label = "Family", sample.order = OrderITS, taxa.order = "Phylum", max.label = 1200, low = "#331900", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000")

Variable_ITSC <- tax_glom(Variable_ITS, taxrank = "Class", NArm = FALSE)
plot_heatmap(Variable_ITSC, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray", taxa.label = "Class", sample.order = OrderITS, taxa.order = "Phylum", max.label = 1200, low = "#331900", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000")

Variable_ITSP <- tax_glom(Variable_ITS, taxrank = "Phylum", NArm = FALSE)
plot_heatmap(Variable_ITSP, method = "PCoA", distance = "bray", taxa.label = "Phylum", sample.order = OrderITS, taxa.order = "Phylum", max.label = 1200, low = "#331900", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000")

plot_heatmap(Variable_ITS, method = "PCoA", distance = "jaccard", taxa.label = "ASV", max.label = 1200, low = "#1A0D00", high = "#FFCC99", na.value = "#FF0000") +
    labs(title = "Heatmap of Differentially Abundant ASVs (ITS data)",
         x = "Substrate")+
    scale_y_discrete("ASVs", labels=NULL)
ggsave("ASV_ITS_DAAHeat.png", device = png, width = 1200, height = 700, units = "px", dpi = 144, limitsize = FALSE)
```

```{r}
# Cleanup
rm(Difflist_16S_DESeq, Difflist_16S_ADAPT, Order16S, Core_16S, Variable_16S, Variable_16SG, Variable_16SF, Variable_16SC, Variable_16SP, Core_ITS, Variable_ITS, Variable_ITSG, Variable_ITSF, Variable_ITSC, Variable_ITSP, OrderITS)
```

## 

## Phylogenetic comparison

```{perl}
print "Hello from Perl!\n";}
```

To construct a phylogentic tree, we need to have a taxonomy table from only the taxa/ASVs that are found in the citydeal experiment. The taxonomy file we loaded earlier (to put in phyloseq object) also contained other ASVs.

```         
{r} Taxonomy_full <- read.csv("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS_ASV_Tables/ITS_spring23_ASV_taxonomy.csv", row.names = 1, sep = ";")  Taxonomy_citydeal <- df_subset <- Taxonomy_full[rownames(Taxonomy_full) %in% rownames(phy_rare@sam_data), ]  write.csv(Taxonomy_citydeal, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS_ASV_Tables/Taxonomy_table_Citydeal.txt")}
```

Okay, now we made a taxonomy table only containing the ASVs found in the Citydeal experiment. let's see if we can make a tree. For this we use taxonomy_to_tree.pl from <https://link.springer.com/article/10.1007/s13225-018-0401-0#Sec10> (sup. 3). Sadly I can't run a perl script here because I'm not an adiministrator, so I ran it in the command prompt (perl taxonomy_to_tree.pl path/Taxonomy_table_Citydeal.txt

C:\Users\sjoerd.gremmen\Documents\R-scripts\perl\bin\> perl taxonomy_to_tree.pl "G:\My Drive\CityDeal\ITS\_ASV_Tables\Taxonomy\_table_Citydeal_ASVNAMEATEND_NoFALSE.txt" \> "G:\My Drive\CityDeal\ITS-Sjoerd\ITS-11912\\ITS_tree_citydeal-TEST2.nwk

## Add tree to Phyloseq

```{r}
citydeal_tree <- ape::read.tree("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/ITS_tree_citydeal-used.nwk") #plot(citydeal_tree)  tree_taxa <- citydeal_tree$tip.label citydeal_tree$tip.label <- gsub('\"\"\"', '', tree_taxa) physeq_taxa <- taxa_names(phy_rare)  tree_taxa setdiff(tree_taxa,physeq_taxa)  phy_tree(phy_rare) <- citydeal_tree  rm(tree_taxa, physeq_taxa, citydeal_tree)}`
```

## SIMPER

### ITS

This calculate similarity percentages between two groups using Bray Curtis dissimilarities

```{r}
# The simper analysis is quite heavy and takes long. So I've decided to remove all ASVs that are not present in at least 3 samples, with the expectation that they will not have a big influence on the Bray-Curtis. Ofcourse as a group together, they can have a big difference, but the analysis wouldn't have picked that up anyways. 

prevalence_threshold <- 0.006  # 0.5% prevalence, so at least in 3 samples 
keep_asvs <- rowSums(otu_table(phy_rare) > 0) / ncol(otu_table(phy_rare)) >= prevalence_threshold  asv_filtered <- as.data.frame(otu_table(phy_rare)[keep_asvs,])  #standard permutations is 1000, I've decided to lower it for now to see how long this analysis takes. 
simper_ITS_city <- simper(t(asv_filtered), sample_data(phy_rare)$city, permutations = 100)}
```

Making one big dataframe, this allows easy comparisons

```{r}
simper_comparisons <- names(simper_ITS_city) 
simper_comparisons 
Results_simper_its_city <- c()  
for(i in 1:length(simper_comparisons)) { 
  require(tidyverse) 
  temp <- summary(simper_ITS_city)[as.character(simper_comparisons[i])] %>% as.data.frame() 
  colnames(temp) <- gsub( paste(simper_comparisons[i],".", sep = ""), "", colnames(temp)) 
  temp <- temp %>% mutate(Comparison = simper_comparisons[i], Position = row_number()) %>%   rownames_to_column(var = "ASVs") 
  Results_simper_its_city <- rbind(Results_simper_its_city, temp) }
```

Filter on statistically relevant only

```{r}
Results_simper_its %>% filter(p <= 0.05) %>% select(ASVs, average, Comparison, Position)}


```

Adding up the contributions of all species in each pairwise combination:

```{r}
Results_simper_its %>% group_by(Comparison) %>% summarize(sum.average = sum(average))}
```

some visualisation: Cumulative

```{r}
p1 <- ggplot(data = Results_simper_its_city, aes(x = Position, y = cumsum)) + 
  geom_line(aes(colour = Comparison)) + 
  theme_bw()  
p1 
p2 <- ggplot(data = Results_simper_its_city, aes(x = Position, y = average)) +
  geom_line(aes(colour = Comparison)) + 
  theme_bw()  
p2 
library(ggpubr)  
ggarrange(p1, p2, common.legend = TRUE)  
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/simper_graphs_city.png", width = 15, height = 5, units = "in", dpi = 600)  
write.csv(Results_simper_its_city, "G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/simper_results_ITS_city.csv")}
```

Making a bar graph to show the most important differentiation ASVs

```{r}
# Select top ASVs for each comparison #
top_n_asvs <- 10  # Adjust as needed 
#top_asvs <- Results_simper_its_city %>% #  group_by(Comparison) %>% 
#  top_n(top_n_asvs, average) %>% #  ungroup() ## This makes a total figure, Can be done for each thing as well:  # Filter top 10 ASVs per comparison top_10_asvs <- Results_simper_its %>%   filter(Position <= 10)  # Keep only top 10 ASVs for each comparison  # Plot ggplot(top_10_asvs, aes(x = reorder(ASVs, average), y = average, fill = Comparison)) +   geom_bar(stat = "identity", position = "dodge") +   coord_flip() +  # Horizontal bars   labs(x = "ASVs", y = "Average Contribution", title = "Top Contributing ASVs by Group") +   theme_bw() +   scale_fill_brewer(palette = "Set3")  # Nice colors for comparisons ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/simper_graphs_topology.png", width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)}
```

And a heathmap

```{r}
library(pheatmap) library(tidyr)  # Prepare data for heatmap heatmap_data <- Results_simper_its %>%   select(ASVs, Comparison, average) %>%   tidyr::spread(Comparison, average) %>%   column_to_rownames("ASVs")  # Generate heatmap pheatmap(heatmap_data,           scale = "row",  # Normalize across ASVs          clustering_distance_rows = "euclidean",          clustering_distance_cols = "euclidean",          clustering_method = "complete",          color = colorRampPalette(c("blue", "white", "red"))(50),          main = "Heatmap of Top 10 ASV Contributions") 
ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/simper_heathmap_ITS_topology.png", width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)}
```

Maybe a boxplot with average abundance per ASV is a nice way to show it at some point

## For each typology comparison see which ones are relevant

load data

```{r}
simper_ITS <- read.csv("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/simper_results_ITS_topology.csv")
```

add taxonomic data to it

```{r}
ITS_Taxonomy <- read.csv("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS_ASV_Tables/Taxonomy_table_Citydeal.txt", sep = ",")
simper_its_taxonomy <- merge(simper_ITS, ITS_Taxonomy, by = "ASVs", all.x = T)
```

Subselect the ones with p\<0.05 and subset dataframes

```{r}
relevant_simper_ITS <- simper_its_taxonomy[simper_its_taxonomy$p <= 0.05,]
relevant_simper_ITS_list <- split(relevant_simper_ITS, relevant_simper_ITS$Comparison)

```

chatGPT help for visualisation

head(relevant_simper_ITS_list[[1]], n=1)

```{r}
library(tidyverse)

# Function to process a dataframe
process_df <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Class) %>%
    summarise(ASV_Count = n()) %>%
    mutate(Relative_Abundance = ASV_Count / sum(ASV_Count),
           Dataset = dataset_name)  # Assign the dataset name
}

# Apply function to all dataframes in the list while keeping their names
df_list_processed <- Map(process_df, relevant_simper_ITS_list, names(relevant_simper_ITS_list))

# Combine all into one dataframe
df_combined <- bind_rows(df_list_processed)
```

plot

```{r}
ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = factor(Dataset), y = Relative_Abundance, fill = Class)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  mytheme +
  labs(x = "Dataset", y = "Proportion of ASVs", fill = "Class", title = "Community Structure at Class Level") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/test_bargraph_Class.png", width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

```{r}
process_df_absolute <- function(df, dataset_name) {
  df %>%
    group_by(Class) %>%
    summarise(ASV_Count = n()) %>%
    mutate(Dataset = dataset_name)  # Assign dataset name
}

# Apply function to all dataframes in the list
df_list_processed <- Map(process_df_absolute, relevant_simper_ITS_list, names(relevant_simper_ITS_list))
# Combine all into a single dataframe
df_combined <- bind_rows(df_list_processed)
```

```{r}
ggplot(df_combined, aes(x = factor(Dataset), y = ASV_Count, fill = Class)) +
  geom_bar(stat = "identity") +
  mytheme +
  labs(x = "Dataset", y = "Proportion of ASVs", fill = "Class", title = "Community Structure at Class Level") +
  theme(axis.text.x = element_text(angle = 90, hjust = 1))

ggsave("G:/My Drive/CityDeal/ITS-Sjoerd/ITS-11912/Simper_results/absolute_bargraph_Class.png", width = 15, height = 10, units = "in", dpi = 600)
```

## And the same for 16S

preparing data

```{r}
# The simper analysis is quite heavy and takes long. So I've decided to remove all ASVs that are not present in at least 3 samples, with the expectation that they will not have a big influence on the Bray-Curtis. Ofcourse as a group together, they can have a big difference, but the analysis wouldn't have picked that up anyways. 

prevalence_threshold <- 0.005  # 0.5% prevalence, so at least in 3 samples 
keep_asvs <- rowSums(otu_table(physeq_filtered_16s) > 0) / ncol(otu_table(physeq_filtered_16s)) >= prevalence_threshold 
asv_filtered <- as.data.frame(otu_table(physeq_filtered_16s)[keep_asvs,])  #standard permutations is 1000, I've decided to lower it for now to see how long this analysis takes. 

simper_16_topology <- simper(t(asv_filtered), sample_data(physeq_filtered_16s)$topology, permutations = 100)
```

```{r}
simper_comparisons <- names(simper_16_topology) 
simper_comparisons 
Results_simper_16 <- c()  
for(i in 1:length(simper_16_topology)) { 
  require(tidyverse) 
  temp <- summary(simper_ITS_city)[as.character(simper_comparisons[i])] %>% as.data.frame() 
  colnames(temp) <- gsub( paste(simper_comparisons[i],".", sep = ""), "", colnames(temp)) 
  temp <- temp %>% mutate(Comparison = simper_comparisons[i], Position = row_number()) %>%   rownames_to_column(var = "ASVs") 
  Results_simper_16_city <- rbind(Results_simper_16, temp) }
```
